steps.title: Match the intercepts
steps.content: The graph has $x$-intercepts at $x=-5$, $x=0$, and $x=4$. So the factored equation must include the factors $x$, $x-4$, and $x+5$.

steps.title: Use the end behavior
steps.content: The graph is below the $x$-axis for very large positive and negative values of $x$. With a negative leading coefficient, that means the polynomial has even degree.

steps.title: Choose the even-degree option
steps.content: The option with roots $-5$, $0$, and $4$ and even total degree is $$ y=-\frac{1}{10}x(x-4){(x+5)}^2 $$ The squared factor also matches the bounce at $x=-5$. The answer is B.
